title: Infer Safety-Car/VSC Regimes From Lap-Wide Distortions
summary: Public F1 strategy work treats FCY state as one of the highest-value pit predictors, and distinguishes first-lap SC/VSC from later laps because most opportunistic stops happen immediately.
rationale: Public F1 strategy work treats FCY state as one of the highest-value pit predictors, and distinguishes first-lap SC/VSC from later laps because most opportunistic stops happen immediately. Your current features approximate pit waves, but they do not explicitly infer neutralization state from the available tabular signals.
implementation_hint: Build race-lap proxy features from available columns only: field median LapTime inflation vs recent race baseline, field LapTime_Delta inflation, prior-lap pit-wave size, and continuation flags. Create a 3-5 state inferred regime feature such as normal/first-wave/continuation/late-wave, then either add it to the main model or train a separate expert for suspected neutralization laps and rank-blend OOF predictions.
expected_effect: Should improve recall on strategically opportunistic stops and reduce noise from treating neutralized laps like normal degradation-driven laps.
risk: Proxy labels may be noisy because the dataset lacks explicit SC/VSC annotations; overfitting to extreme lap-time spikes is possible on wet or disrupted laps.
